text.title: api-monitoring-logging Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-monitoring-logging as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Calling logger.info(request.json()) to log the full request body.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Request bodies routinely contain passwords, tokens, credit card numbers, and PII. Logging them sends secrets into log aggregation platforms with multi-year retention windows. Use field-level redaction; log only safe metadata fields.

failure-mode.description: Wiring OTel tracing without setting a sampler.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a sampler, every request produces a trace. At 1000 RPS, this generates 86 million spans per day. Storage costs explode and Tempo/Jaeger performance degrades. Always set OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER before deploying.

failure-mode.description: Forgetting to propagate trace context in Celery / queue workers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Async tasks that don't extract W3C traceparent from the queue message produce detached trace fragments. The parent request's trace shows a queue publish event but no worker activity. Distributed traces become useless for debugging async workflows.
